comment: Demo policy: no hardcoded video URLs — they rot and none were verified. Each exercise ships coaching cues plus a demo_search string. The app renders a 'Watch demo' button linking to https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=<urlencoded demo_search>. User can pin a preferred video URL per exercise in settings; pinned URLs are user-supplied data.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
cues: Stand mid-bar, shins near handles
Brace hard before the pull — big breath into the belt line
Push the floor away; hips and shoulders rise together
Lock out tall, no lean-back
exercises.safety: Straps on top sets only, from week 3 on.

exercises.name: Axle Clean & Strict Press
cues: Continental clean: pull to belt, catch on belly/forearms, reset grip, pop to shoulders
Elbows slightly forward in the rack
Strict press: squeeze glutes, ribs down, press around the chin then through
exercises.safety: Keep continentals light the first month — no bicep yank on the catch. This movement and sloppy bag work are the two classic biceps-tear mechanisms.
exercises.demo_search: axle continental clean and press strongman

exercises.name: Axle Push Press
cues: Shallow dip — knees forward, torso vertical
Drive through the floor, bar leaves shoulders on leg power
Punch under and lock
exercises.safety: Same load as strict target; the legs buy the extra reps.

exercises.name: DB Bench Press
cues: Shoulder blades pinned to the bench
Elbows ~45 degrees
Press to mid-chest lockout

exercises.name: Heavy DB Row
cues: Hand and knee on bench, flat back
Row to the hip, not the armpit
Control the bottom — no bounce

exercises.name: DB Split Squat
cues: Long stance, rear heel up
Drop straight down, front shin near vertical
Drive through the front foot

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry
cues: One DB at the side like a suitcase
Hips and shoulders level — the work is refusing to lean
Short fast steps

exercises.name: Sandbag Bear-Hug Squat
cues: Wrap and crush the bag to the chest
Sit between the knees
Stand through the mid-foot

exercises.name: Sandbag to Shoulder
cues: Deadlift bag to the lap
Hug it high on the chest
Violent hip extension throws it to the shoulder — arms guide, never curl
exercises.safety: NEVER reverse-curl the bag up. Lap, hug, hips. This rule is non-negotiable — it is the biceps-tear mechanism.

exercises.name: Sandbag over Bar
cues: Smith bar pinned 48-52 inches
Lap the bag, hug, extend hips and roll it over the bar
Reset fully each rep

exercises.name: Sandbag Bear-Hug Carry
cues: Crush grip on the bag, chest tall as possible
Breathe shallow and fast at the top of the brace
Short quick steps

exercises.name: Trap Bar Farmer's Carry
cues: Deadlift it up, settle one beat
Tall posture, eyes forward
Quick feet; fight the wobble, don't chase it
exercises.safety: Straps fine once grip is the only limiter. Q2+: timed runs — drop time before adding load.
exercises.note: Handle position differs from true farmer's handles; carry stimulus is close.

exercises.name: Axle Deadlift — Double Overhand
cues: Double overhand, no hook, no straps
Crush the bar before it leaves the floor
Set ends when the grip opens, not when the back tires
exercises.safety: Strapless permanently — this slot IS the grip training.

exercises.name: KB Swing
cues: Hinge, don't squat
Snap the hips; the bell floats to chest height
Let it fall, hinge to meet it

exercises.name: Tricep-Bar Skull-Crusher
cues: Elbows fixed, lower to forehead/just behind
Press without flaring
exercises.demo_search: lying tricep extension ez bar form

exercises.name: Curl-Bar Curl
cues: Elbows pinned to sides
Full range, controlled negative — the negative is the tendon work
exercises.note: Biceps insurance for event days, not vanity volume.

exercises.name: DB Clean & Press (axle substitute)
cues: Clean both bells to the shoulders once
Strict press the prescribed reps

exercises.name: Smith Machine Squat (bag substitute)
cues: Feet slightly forward of the bar line
Control depth to parallel or below
exercises.note: Stopgap until the sandbag arrives; Smith stays accessory-only afterward — fixed bar path has poor carryover to strongman events.
